scenario: Granite Bank evaluates which BPMN event icons carry an implementation marker in Camunda 8.8 before standardizing on a modeling palette for 60 microservices that orchestrate payment workflows. The architect focuses on cancel events to confirm whether they can appear in executable models.
question: Which cancel event variant in the BPMN coverage page appears without an implementation marker in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Only the cancel boundary event icon appears without an implementation marker, while the cancel end event icon carries the implementation marker on the events table
Only the cancel end event icon appears without an implementation marker, while the cancel boundary event icon carries the implementation marker on the events table
Only the cancel throw event icon appears without an implementation marker, while the cancel boundary event icon carries the implementation marker on the events table
Both the cancel boundary event icon and the cancel end event icon appear without an implementation marker on the BPMN coverage events table

a.text: Incorrect. The cancel end event icon is also rendered without an implementation marker in the BPMN coverage events table, so this split between boundary and end is not what the table shows.

b.text: Incorrect. The cancel boundary event icon is also rendered without an implementation marker in the BPMN coverage events table, so this split between end and boundary is not what the table shows.

c.text: Incorrect. The BPMN coverage events table does not include a cancel throw event row at all, so it is not the variant rendered without an implementation marker.

d.text: Correct. The BPMN coverage events table renders both the cancel boundary event icon and the cancel end event icon without an implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 does not execute either variant.

explanation: Cancel events appear in the BPMN coverage events table as boundary and end variants. Both are rendered without the implementation marker, signaling that Camunda 8 does not execute either cancel event variant.
